# Thales of Miletus

*625 BCE – 545 BCE · Greek*

The first name of Greek philosophy: astronomer, geometer and sage of Miletus, who sought in water the principle of all things. Tradition credits him with the fall into a well to a servant girl's laughter, and with the fortune made on olive presses: the two faces of philosophy's 'uselessness'.

> Stub profile: only this mini-card exists so far; the sourced axis positions remain to be authored. Contributions welcome.
